coffeeDescription: Chroast is a specialty roastery from Hradec Kralove, Czech Republic, founded 2018. Their name comes from the founders family name (Chmelik) + roast. Kafe protoze koks je drahej — 50% Colombia + 50% Brazil regional blend, mixed processing (Natural + Washed). Light roast (195-202C). Flavor: apple, chocolate, caramel. Designed as an accessible daily office espresso. 1kg bags. Roasted 26.01.2026.
notes: Office anchor coffee. Weird good — normal chocolate-forward, easy to dial, very stable and forgiving. Flat but pleasant, nothing wild. Perfect daily driver for office — colleagues enjoy it, no drama. Grind 1.75 on Eureka, 20-24s shots.

coffeeDescription: Filtr is a Czech specialty coffee roastery. Kenya Kainamui AA — washed process, grade AA. Flavor: rhubarb, blackcurrant. Roasted 5.1.2026, best before 5.7.2026. Perfectly rested for brewing.
notes: V60 filter. 18g/300g water (1:16.7), 90C, bloom 45g/40-45s. Best at 90C vs 92C — 92C compressed acidity into blunt rhubarb, 90C gave sharper cleaner red currant forward. High clarity, bright structured acidity, very responsive to temperature changes.

coffeeDescription: Gift bag from Emirates Airlines and Praha Letiste (Prague Airport) — Christmas 2025 promotional coffee. Unknown roastery and origin. Commercial blend, not specialty.
notes: Airport gift. Likely similar decoration-tier to Idee Kaffee. May try as French press filler eventually.

coffeeName: Costa Rica San Ramon de Altura
flavorNotes: Floral perfume, figs, honey, white tea
coffeeDescription: DOT Coffee — Kava vyladena do puntiku (Coffee tuned to perfection), a Czech specialty roastery. Costa Rica San Ramon de Altura — farmer association, farm San Ramon Cafe de Altura, region Tarrazú, 1400m, varieties Caturra and Catuai. Flavor: floral perfume, figs, honey, white tea. 100% Arabica, 250g. Roasted 21.01.2026.
notes: Brewed V60. Classic elegant Costa Rican filter — Earl Grey / white tea character, silky body, low-medium sweetness, tea-like acidity, short clean finish. Also tried Honey Mode at 94-95C for more sweetness and fig notes. Second Costa Rica that really impressed.

coffeeDescription: Master Vietnam Espresso — a Vietnamese/Brazilian blend roasted in Czech Republic. Composition: 70% Arabica / 30% Robusta. Dark roast (5/5). Flavor: caramel, chocolate, nuts. 250g. One of the earlier coffees in the journey. Second bag in freezer.
notes: Early period — predates CSV tracking. Unseasoned grinder. Dial-in parameters not reliably recorded. Second bag in freezer — worth retrying with seasoned setup for comparison.

coffeeDescription: Coffeespot is a Moravian specialty roastery (Babice, Czech Republic), roasting on a Loring roaster since 2011. The Vanocni Smes 2025 is their 12th annual limited Christmas blend — Brazil Diamond Santos and Honduras San Andres SHG. 100% Arabica, 250g. Flavor: chocolate, nuts, dried fruit. Roasted 13.11.2025.

flavorNotes: Ripe banana, blueberry, strawberry — strong, fluid, expressive
coffeeDescription: Tarbon is a Czech specialty roastery based in Dvur Kralove nad Labem, roasting under the motto made with love. The A.I is their special blend of selected Vietnamese arabica, processed using experimental anaerobic/fermented methods. Christmas limited edition. Notes of ripe banana, blueberry and strawberry — bold, expressive and fluid in the cup. 100% Arabica, 227g.
notes: Benchmark favourite. Took many shots to reach a pleasant extraction. Once dialed, not very stable shot-to-shot. Distinct banana at the start, tropical fruit throughout, pleasant fluid mouthfeel. Worth the effort but demands patience.

flavorNotes: Ripe banana, blueberry, strawberry — strong, fluid, expressive
coffeeDescription: Tarbon is a Czech specialty roastery based in Dvur Kralove nad Labem, roasting under the motto "made with love". The A.I is their special blend of selected Vietnamese arabica, processed using experimental anaerobic/fermented methods to produce a distinctive sweet-fruit profile. Christmas limited edition (Vanocni prazeni). Notes of ripe banana, blueberry and strawberry — bold, expressive and fluid in the cup. 100% Arabica, 227g.
notes: Benchmark favourite. Took many shots to reach a pleasant extraction — even getting a drinkable shot was hard initially. Once dialed, not very stable shot-to-shot. Distinct banana at the start of the shot, tropical fruit throughout, pleasant fluid mouthfeel. Worth the effort but demands patience.

flavorNotes: Floral, elegant acidity, light body, sweetness arrives late
notes: Occasional / exploratory. Interesting but not a daily driver. Floral, elegant acidity, light body. Lower temp (88C) and tighter ratio. Sweetness comes late.

flavorNotes: Low acidity, calm, mild chocolate — flat when pushed
notes: Support / milk coffee role. Limited verdict. Low acidity, calm, mild chocolate — loses character when pushed. Better suited for milk drinks only.

flavorNotes: Banana, tropical fruit, early sweetness — strong, fluid, expressive
notes: Benchmark favourite. Took many shots to reach a pleasant extraction — even getting a drinkable shot was hard initially. Once dialed, not very stable shot-to-shot. Distinct banana at the start of the shot, tropical fruit throughout, pleasant fluid mouthfeel. Worth the effort but demands patience.
Perfect shot. Clean berry notes with dark chocolate finish. 1:2 at 29s is the sweet spot.
Slightly longer pull brings out more sweetness. Setting 11 was the sweet spot after testing 10.5 and 11.5.
